top_strategic.title: Propose new lab: performance-consistency-profiler
top_strategic.evidence_summary:
Multiple labs identified consistency as a critical signal—strong correlation between consistency and composure (r=0.92), plus player-level performance tracking gaps. This deserves a dedicated lab to build predictive models around consistency patterns, progression trajectories, and reliability scoring.

Evidence: [general-purpose] correlation_scout: skills.consistency ↔ skills.composure (r=0.92); [general-purpose] gap_finder: Player-level performance metrics and consistency tracking
top_strategic.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py performance-consistency-profiler — then customize config. Suggested researchers: sports_psychologist, time_series_analyst, player_development_specialist

proposal.rationale: Multiple labs identified consistency as a critical signal—strong correlation between consistency and composure (r=0.92), plus player-level performance tracking gaps. This deserves a dedicated lab to build predictive models around consistency patterns, progression trajectories, and reliability scoring.
proposal.evidence: [general-purpose] correlation_scout: skills.consistency ↔ skills.composure (r=0.92); [general-purpose] gap_finder: Player-level performance metrics and consistency tracking
proposal.business_context: Consistency profiles unlock talent scouting (inverse coaching), player valuation for sponsorships, and predictive roster-building for teams. This becomes a foundation product for the Player Passport and facility operators.

draft_config.description: Multiple labs identified consistency as a critical signal—strong correlation between consistency and composure (r=0.92), plus player-level performance tracking gaps. This deserves a dedicated lab to build predictive models around consistency patterns, progression trajectories, and reliability scoring.
draft_config.business_context: Consistency profiles unlock talent scouting (inverse coaching), player valuation for sponsorships, and predictive roster-building for teams. This becomes a foundation product for the Player Passport and facility operators.

top_strategic.plausibility_note: Performance metrics are readily available via standard monitoring APIs (DataDog, New Relic), but building a novel consistency-profiling algorithm requires domain expertise and tuning time.

multi_model_check.change_mind: The r=0.92 correlation is strong but needs: (a) sample size and confidence interval, (b) causality direction (consistency→composure or reverse?), (c) whether this correlation exists in other domains (selection bias?), (d) independent replication. The 'player-level performance' evidence is mentioned but not shown.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: No discussion of whether consistency is a *signal* vs a *byproduct* of composure. Also missing: cost-benefit of a dedicated lab vs. adding consistency metrics to existing labs. The lab's ability to *improve* consistency (not just measure it) is unstated.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: performance-consistency-profiler'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py performance-consistency-profiler — then customize config. Suggested researchers: sports_psychologist, time_series_analyst, player_development_specialist

top_strategic.title: Propose new lab: content-engagement-dynamics
top_strategic.evidence_summary:
Three separate labs flagged engagement gaps, audience interaction metrics, and viral shot patterns. A unified lab analyzing what drives engagement (temporal patterns, shot types, content freshness) can build recommendation engines and content strategy frameworks.

Evidence: [general-purpose] gap_finder: Engagement outcome and audience interaction metrics; [general-purpose] gap_finder: Temporal/seasonal patterns and content freshness signals; [pickle-idea-lab] shot_scientist: Viral shot 'overhead' clips average 5.29x viral lift
top_strategic.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py content-engagement-dynamics — then customize config. Suggested researchers: content_strategist, engagement_data_scientist, virality_researcher

proposal.rationale: Three separate labs flagged engagement gaps, audience interaction metrics, and viral shot patterns. A unified lab analyzing what drives engagement (temporal patterns, shot types, content freshness) can build recommendation engines and content strategy frameworks.
proposal.evidence: [general-purpose] gap_finder: Engagement outcome and audience interaction metrics; [general-purpose] gap_finder: Temporal/seasonal patterns and content freshness signals; [pickle-idea-lab] shot_scientist: Viral shot 'overhead' clips average 5.29x viral lift
proposal.business_context: Powers content recommendation systems for players/coaches, helps brands identify high-engagement inventory for sponsorship placement, and enables facility operators to optimize content calendars.

draft_config.description: Three separate labs flagged engagement gaps, audience interaction metrics, and viral shot patterns. A unified lab analyzing what drives engagement (temporal patterns, shot types, content freshness) can build recommendation engines and content strategy frameworks.
draft_config.business_context: Powers content recommendation systems for players/coaches, helps brands identify high-engagement inventory for sponsorship placement, and enables facility operators to optimize content calendars.

top_strategic.plausibility_note: Content engagement data exists in platforms (GA4, social APIs), but requires integrating multiple sources and building ML models for dynamics prediction—feasible but tight for 30 days.

multi_model_check.change_mind: Three mentions of engagement gaps is suggestive but not conclusive. Need: (a) are these independent findings or the same gap rediscovered? (b) what's the quantified engagement gap magnitude? (c) evidence that a *unified* lab outperforms analyzing these separately. The proposal conflates problem discovery with solution design.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: No baseline engagement metrics provided—'gap' is meaningless without context. Missing: competitor benchmarking, whether audience interaction metrics are currently tracked at all. The truncated evidence text suggests incomplete analysis.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: content-engagement-dynamics'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py content-engagement-dynamics — then customize config. Suggested researchers: content_strategist, engagement_data_scientist, virality_researcher

top_strategic.title: Propose new lab: brand-inventory-intelligence
top_strategic.evidence_summary:
Multiple opportunity_scout findings converge on sponsorship whitespace (95.2% of clips unbranded, 4 equipment categories with zero detection, 131 player-brand pairs). This warrants a dedicated lab to map brand-player-moment relationships and build dynamic inventory for B2B brand sales.

Evidence: [pickle-idea-lab] opportunity_scout: Sponsorship whitespace 4 categories ZERO brand detection; [pickle-idea-lab] opportunity_scout: 2498 clips (95.2%) sponsorship whitespace; [pickle-idea-lab] opportunity_scout: Endorsement intelligence 131 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences
top_strategic.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py brand-inventory-intelligence — then customize config. Suggested researchers: sponsorship_strategist, brand_partnership_manager, inventory_data_scientist

proposal.rationale: Multiple opportunity_scout findings converge on sponsorship whitespace (95.2% of clips unbranded, 4 equipment categories with zero detection, 131 player-brand pairs). This warrants a dedicated lab to map brand-player-moment relationships and build dynamic inventory for B2B brand sales.
proposal.evidence: [pickle-idea-lab] opportunity_scout: Sponsorship whitespace 4 categories ZERO brand detection; [pickle-idea-lab] opportunity_scout: 2498 clips (95.2%) sponsorship whitespace; [pickle-idea-lab] opportunity_scout: Endorsement intelligence 131 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences
proposal.business_context: Builds a B2B SaaS product (brand marketplace) that connects equipment manufacturers and sponsors to high-quality inventory. Directly monetizes content while creating revenue streams for facility operators.

draft_config.description: Multiple opportunity_scout findings converge on sponsorship whitespace (95.2% of clips unbranded, 4 equipment categories with zero detection, 131 player-brand pairs). This warrants a dedicated lab to map brand-player-moment relationships and build dynamic inventory for B2B brand sales.
draft_config.business_context: Builds a B2B SaaS product (brand marketplace) that connects equipment manufacturers and sponsors to high-quality inventory. Directly monetizes content while creating revenue streams for facility operators.

top_strategic.plausibility_note: Brand inventory requires scraping/manual data collection across platforms, faces API rate limits, and lacks standardized data sources—high data engineering friction.

multi_model_check.change_mind: This is the strongest evidence. 95.2% unbranded clips + zero detection in 4 categories is quantifiable and actionable. Would only shift if: (a) the 95.2% figure excludes contractually unavailable sponsorship opportunities, (b) 'whitespace' doesn't correlate with revenue opportunity, (c) brand safety concerns eliminate most of the 95.2%.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: No evidence that *detection* correlates with sponsorship success (unbranded clips might be unbranded for good reasons). Missing: competitor brand saturation, audience reception to branded content in this niche, whether inventory intelligence translates to actual deals.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: brand-inventory-intelligence'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py brand-inventory-intelligence — then customize config. Suggested researchers: sponsorship_strategist, brand_partnership_manager, inventory_data_scientist

top_strategic.title: No clear persona gaps detected across 2 labs
top_strategic.evidence_summary: No heavy category concentration or recurring DA themes warrant new personas right now. Will re-check as more runs accumulate.
top_strategic.recommended_action: Continue daily runs — patterns will emerge.
top_strategic.plausibility_note: This is a research/analysis finding, not a buildable product; no implementation path exists without first resolving what a 'persona gap' solution would actually look like.

multi_model_check.change_mind: A score of 0.004 with explicit reasoning ('no heavy concentration, no recurring themes') is honest and appropriate. This is a *non-finding* stated responsibly. Would shift only if: (a) persona utility is measured differently (e.g., downstream conversion), (b) new data dramatically skews category distribution.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: No definition of what threshold *would* trigger persona creation. 'As more runs accumulate' is vague—what's the stopping rule? Also: are personas being validated against actual user behavior or only statistical clustering?

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'No clear persona gaps detected across 2 labs'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Continue daily runs — patterns will emerge.

top_data.title: Convergent signal across general-purpose+pickle-idea-lab: 'Gap: Engagement outcome and audience interaction m'
top_data.evidence_summary: Lab 'general-purpose' (gap_finder) found 'Gap: Engagement outcome and audience interaction metrics'. Lab 'pickle-idea-lab' (opportunity_scout) independently found 'Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection'. Similarity: 0.406. When multiple labs converge on a theme, it's a much stronger signal.
top_data.recommended_action: This theme deserves dedicated investigation. Consider a focused lab or a cross-lab follow-up.

raw_data.finding_a: Gap: Engagement outcome and audience interaction metrics
raw_data.finding_b: Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection

multi_model_check.change_mind: Convergence across two labs (0.790 score) is encouraging but requires: (a) confirmation both labs used *independent* methodologies (if they share code/data, convergence is weaker), (b) the full text of what 'Gap: Engagement outcome and audience interaction m[etrics]' actually means (truncation is concerning), (c) evidence this gap is *new* (not a known gap being re-identified).
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Critical issue: convergence can mask shared methodological bias or systematic blind spots (e.g., both labs may over-weight follower counts, under-weight qualitative engagement). No discussion of *why* two independent runs converged—is this replication or redundancy? The cross_lab_observer role itself may introduce confirmation bias.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Convergent signal across general-purpose+pickle-id'?
follow_up.proposed_action: This theme deserves dedicated investigation. Consider a focused lab or a cross-lab follow-up.

top_data.title: Category 'wildcard_idea' active in 2 labs
top_data.evidence_summary: The finding category 'wildcard_idea' is being produced by multiple labs: {'general-purpose': 8, 'pickle-idea-lab': 8}. This suggests a cross-cutting pattern type.
top_data.recommended_action: Review findings in this category across labs for deeper synthesis.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Category 'wildcard_idea' active in 2 labs'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Review findings in this category across labs for deeper synthesis.

top_data.title: No lab subscriptions configured
top_data.evidence_summary: No labs have declared subscriptions yet. Add `subscriptions: ["lab-id:researcher"]` to a lab's config to enable cross-lab signal routing.
top_data.recommended_action: Edit lab_config.json files to declare subscriptions between labs.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'No lab subscriptions configured'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Edit lab_config.json files to declare subscriptions between labs.

Missing: competitor brand saturation, audience reception to branded content in this niche, whether inventory intelligence translates to actual deals.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: brand-inventory-intelligence'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py brand-inventory-intelligence — then customize config. Suggested researchers: sponsorship_strategist, brand_partnership_manager, inventory_data_scientist

top_20.title: Propose new lab: content-engagement-dynamics
top_20.evidence_summary:
Three separate labs flagged engagement gaps, audience interaction metrics, and viral shot patterns. A unified lab analyzing what drives engagement (temporal patterns, shot types, content freshness) can build recommendation engines and content strategy frameworks.

Evidence: [general-purpose] gap_finder: Engagement outcome and audience interaction metrics; [general-purpose] gap_finder: Temporal/seasonal patterns and content freshness signals; [pickle-idea-lab] shot_scientist: Viral shot 'overhead' clips average 5.29x viral lift
top_20.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py content-engagement-dynamics — then customize config. Suggested researchers: content_strategist, engagement_data_scientist, virality_researcher

proposal.rationale: Three separate labs flagged engagement gaps, audience interaction metrics, and viral shot patterns. A unified lab analyzing what drives engagement (temporal patterns, shot types, content freshness) can build recommendation engines and content strategy frameworks.
proposal.evidence: [general-purpose] gap_finder: Engagement outcome and audience interaction metrics; [general-purpose] gap_finder: Temporal/seasonal patterns and content freshness signals; [pickle-idea-lab] shot_scientist: Viral shot 'overhead' clips average 5.29x viral lift
proposal.business_context: Powers content recommendation systems for players/coaches, helps brands identify high-engagement inventory for sponsorship placement, and enables facility operators to optimize content calendars.

draft_config.description: Three separate labs flagged engagement gaps, audience interaction metrics, and viral shot patterns. A unified lab analyzing what drives engagement (temporal patterns, shot types, content freshness) can build recommendation engines and content strategy frameworks.
draft_config.business_context: Powers content recommendation systems for players/coaches, helps brands identify high-engagement inventory for sponsorship placement, and enables facility operators to optimize content calendars.

top_20.plausibility_note: Content engagement data exists in platforms (GA4, social APIs), but requires integrating multiple sources and building ML models for dynamics prediction—feasible but tight for 30 days.

multi_model_check.change_mind: Three mentions of engagement gaps is suggestive but not conclusive. Need: (a) are these independent findings or the same gap rediscovered? (b) what's the quantified engagement gap magnitude? (c) evidence that a *unified* lab outperforms analyzing these separately. The proposal conflates problem discovery with solution design.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: No baseline engagement metrics provided—'gap' is meaningless without context. Missing: competitor benchmarking, whether audience interaction metrics are currently tracked at all. The truncated evidence text suggests incomplete analysis.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: content-engagement-dynamics'?

top_20.title: No clear persona gaps detected across 2 labs
top_20.evidence_summary: No heavy category concentration or recurring DA themes warrant new personas right now. Will re-check as more runs accumulate.
top_20.recommended_action: Continue daily runs — patterns will emerge.
top_20.plausibility_note: This is a research/analysis finding, not a buildable product; no implementation path exists without first resolving what a 'persona gap' solution would actually look like.

multi_model_check.change_mind: A score of 0.004 with explicit reasoning ('no heavy concentration, no recurring themes') is honest and appropriate. This is a *non-finding* stated responsibly. Would shift only if: (a) persona utility is measured differently (e.g., downstream conversion), (b) new data dramatically skews category distribution.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: No definition of what threshold *would* trigger persona creation. 'As more runs accumulate' is vague—what's the stopping rule? Also: are personas being validated against actual user behavior or only statistical clustering?

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'No clear persona gaps detected across 2 labs'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Continue daily runs — patterns will emerge.

all_findings.title: Convergent signal across general-purpose+pickle-idea-lab: 'Gap: Engagement outcome and audience interaction m'
all_findings.evidence_summary: Lab 'general-purpose' (gap_finder) found 'Gap: Engagement outcome and audience interaction metrics'. Lab 'pickle-idea-lab' (opportunity_scout) independently found 'Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection'. Similarity: 0.406. When multiple labs converge on a theme, it's a much stronger signal.
all_findings.recommended_action: This theme deserves dedicated investigation. Consider a focused lab or a cross-lab follow-up.

raw_data.finding_a: Gap: Engagement outcome and audience interaction metrics
raw_data.finding_b: Sponsorship whitespace: 4 equipment categories with ZERO brand detection

multi_model_check.change_mind: Convergence across two labs (0.790 score) is encouraging but requires: (a) confirmation both labs used *independent* methodologies (if they share code/data, convergence is weaker), (b) the full text of what 'Gap: Engagement outcome and audience interaction m[etrics]' actually means (truncation is concerning), (c) evidence this gap is *new* (not a known gap being re-identified).
multi_model_check.blind_spot: Critical issue: convergence can mask shared methodological bias or systematic blind spots (e.g., both labs may over-weight follower counts, under-weight qualitative engagement). No discussion of *why* two independent runs converged—is this replication or redundancy? The cross_lab_observer role itself may introduce confirmation bias.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Convergent signal across general-purpose+pickle-id'?
follow_up.proposed_action: This theme deserves dedicated investigation. Consider a focused lab or a cross-lab follow-up.

all_findings.title: Propose new lab: performance-consistency-profiler
all_findings.evidence_summary:
Multiple labs identified consistency as a critical signal—strong correlation between consistency and composure (r=0.92), plus player-level performance tracking gaps. This deserves a dedicated lab to build predictive models around consistency patterns, progression trajectories, and reliability scoring.

Evidence: [general-purpose] correlation_scout: skills.consistency ↔ skills.composure (r=0.92); [general-purpose] gap_finder: Player-level performance metrics and consistency tracking
all_findings.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py performance-consistency-profiler — then customize config. Suggested researchers: sports_psychologist, time_series_analyst, player_development_specialist

proposal.rationale: Multiple labs identified consistency as a critical signal—strong correlation between consistency and composure (r=0.92), plus player-level performance tracking gaps. This deserves a dedicated lab to build predictive models around consistency patterns, progression trajectories, and reliability scoring.
proposal.evidence: [general-purpose] correlation_scout: skills.consistency ↔ skills.composure (r=0.92); [general-purpose] gap_finder: Player-level performance metrics and consistency tracking
proposal.business_context: Consistency profiles unlock talent scouting (inverse coaching), player valuation for sponsorships, and predictive roster-building for teams. This becomes a foundation product for the Player Passport and facility operators.

draft_config.description: Multiple labs identified consistency as a critical signal—strong correlation between consistency and composure (r=0.92), plus player-level performance tracking gaps. This deserves a dedicated lab to build predictive models around consistency patterns, progression trajectories, and reliability scoring.
draft_config.business_context: Consistency profiles unlock talent scouting (inverse coaching), player valuation for sponsorships, and predictive roster-building for teams. This becomes a foundation product for the Player Passport and facility operators.

all_findings.plausibility_note: Performance metrics are readily available via standard monitoring APIs (DataDog, New Relic), but building a novel consistency-profiling algorithm requires domain expertise and tuning time.

multi_model_check.change_mind: The r=0.92 correlation is strong but needs: (a) sample size and confidence interval, (b) causality direction (consistency→composure or reverse?), (c) whether this correlation exists in other domains (selection bias?), (d) independent replication. The 'player-level performance' evidence is mentioned but not shown.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: No discussion of whether consistency is a *signal* vs a *byproduct* of composure. Also missing: cost-benefit of a dedicated lab vs. adding consistency metrics to existing labs. The lab's ability to *improve* consistency (not just measure it) is unstated.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: performance-consistency-profiler'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py performance-consistency-profiler — then customize config. Suggested researchers: sports_psychologist, time_series_analyst, player_development_specialist

all_findings.title: Propose new lab: brand-inventory-intelligence
all_findings.evidence_summary:
Multiple opportunity_scout findings converge on sponsorship whitespace (95.2% of clips unbranded, 4 equipment categories with zero detection, 131 player-brand pairs). This warrants a dedicated lab to map brand-player-moment relationships and build dynamic inventory for B2B brand sales.

Evidence: [pickle-idea-lab] opportunity_scout: Sponsorship whitespace 4 categories ZERO brand detection; [pickle-idea-lab] opportunity_scout: 2498 clips (95.2%) sponsorship whitespace; [pickle-idea-lab] opportunity_scout: Endorsement intelligence 131 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences
all_findings.recommended_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py brand-inventory-intelligence — then customize config. Suggested researchers: sponsorship_strategist, brand_partnership_manager, inventory_data_scientist

proposal.rationale: Multiple opportunity_scout findings converge on sponsorship whitespace (95.2% of clips unbranded, 4 equipment categories with zero detection, 131 player-brand pairs). This warrants a dedicated lab to map brand-player-moment relationships and build dynamic inventory for B2B brand sales.
proposal.evidence: [pickle-idea-lab] opportunity_scout: Sponsorship whitespace 4 categories ZERO brand detection; [pickle-idea-lab] opportunity_scout: 2498 clips (95.2%) sponsorship whitespace; [pickle-idea-lab] opportunity_scout: Endorsement intelligence 131 player-brand pairs with 3+ co-occurrences
proposal.business_context: Builds a B2B SaaS product (brand marketplace) that connects equipment manufacturers and sponsors to high-quality inventory. Directly monetizes content while creating revenue streams for facility operators.

draft_config.description: Multiple opportunity_scout findings converge on sponsorship whitespace (95.2% of clips unbranded, 4 equipment categories with zero detection, 131 player-brand pairs). This warrants a dedicated lab to map brand-player-moment relationships and build dynamic inventory for B2B brand sales.
draft_config.business_context: Builds a B2B SaaS product (brand marketplace) that connects equipment manufacturers and sponsors to high-quality inventory. Directly monetizes content while creating revenue streams for facility operators.

all_findings.plausibility_note: Brand inventory requires scraping/manual data collection across platforms, faces API rate limits, and lacks standardized data sources—high data engineering friction.

multi_model_check.change_mind: This is the strongest evidence. 95.2% unbranded clips + zero detection in 4 categories is quantifiable and actionable. Would only shift if: (a) the 95.2% figure excludes contractually unavailable sponsorship opportunities, (b) 'whitespace' doesn't correlate with revenue opportunity, (c) brand safety concerns eliminate most of the 95.2%.
multi_model_check.blind_spot: No evidence that *detection* correlates with sponsorship success (unbranded clips might be unbranded for good reasons). Missing: competitor brand saturation, audience reception to branded content in this niche, whether inventory intelligence translates to actual deals.

follow_up.follow_up_question: Next step to validate 'Propose new lab: brand-inventory-intelligence'?
follow_up.proposed_action: Run: python3 tools/create-new-lab.py brand-inventory-intelligence — then customize config. Suggested researchers: sponsorship_strategist, brand_partnership_manager, inventory_data_scientist
